nodes.standard_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.original_text_name: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
nodes.context: Jihadist group that Boko Haram formerly aligned itself with.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The group formerly aligned itself with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant.
edges.context: Boko Haram was formerly aligned with ISIL.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: Boko Haram and ISIL were initially allies. However, the two groups became enemies due to territorial disputes, because ISIL tried to conquer the zones which were under Boko Haram's control.
edges.context: Boko Haram and ISIL became enemies due to territorial disputes.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the group split, resulting in the emergence of a hostile faction known as the Islamic State's West Africa Province.
edges.context: ISWAP emerged as a hostile faction from a split in Boko Haram in 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: Group: Ansaru (2009–2012)
edges.context: Ansaru was a group that was part of Boko Haram from 2009 to 2012.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Taliban
edges.context: Taliban is listed as a non-state ally of Boko Haram.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)
edges.context: Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb was an ally of Boko Haram from 2007 to 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Ansar Dine (2012–2013)
edges.context: Ansar Dine was an ally of Boko Haram from 2012 to 2013.

edges.target: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa (2012–2013)
edges.context: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa was an ally of Boko Haram from 2012 to 2013.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Al-Mulathameen (2013)
edges.context: Al-Mulathameen was an ally of Boko Haram in 2013.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2015, Shekau pledged allegiance to ISIS self-styled caliph Abu Bakr Al-Baghdadi and became "West African Province" (Wilayat Garb Ifriqiyah), i.e. the West African province of the Islamic State.
edges.context: Boko Haram pledged allegiance to ISIL and became its West African province.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the group split, resulting in the emergence of a hostile faction known as the Islamic State's West Africa Province.
edges.context: ISWAP emerged as a hostile faction after a split from Boko Haram.

edges.evidence_quote: These were the "West African Province" which is part of ISIL; "Jamā'at Ahl as-Sunnah lid-Da'wah wa'l-Jihād" (Boko Haram), under Shekau's control; and "Ansaru" which is loyal to al-Qaeda and rejected the caliphate of al-Bagdadi, though it shares his disapproval of the "wide-reaching interpretation of takfir" of Shekau.
edges.context: Ansaru is a faction that split from Boko Haram and is loyal to al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: "Ansaru" which is loyal to al-Qaeda
edges.context: Ansaru is loyal to al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: The increased sophistication of the group led observers to speculate that Boko Haram was affiliated with Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (AQIM), which was active in Niger.
edges.context: Observers speculated that Boko Haram was affiliated with AQIM.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2014, Shekau released a 16-minute video in which he voiced his support for ISIL's leader Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, al-Qaeda's leader Ayman al-Zawahiri and the Afghan Taliban's leader Mullah Omar.
edges.context: Shekau voiced support for the Afghan Taliban's leader.

edges.evidence_quote: They were linked to a number of kidnappings, often reportedly in association with the splinter group Ansaru
edges.context: Boko Haram and Ansaru cooperated in kidnappings.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: On 7 March, Boko Haram's leader Abubakar Shekau pledged allegiance to ISIL via an audio message posted on the organisation's Twitter account.
edges.context: Boko Haram pledged allegiance to ISIL, becoming a province.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The group formerly aligned itself with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant.
edges.context: Boko Haram was formerly aligned with ISIL.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the group split, resulting in the emergence of a hostile faction known as the Islamic State's West Africa Province.
edges.context: ISWAP is a hostile faction that split from Boko Haram.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The group formerly aligned itself with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant.
edges.context: Boko Haram was formerly aligned with ISIL.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the group split, resulting in the emergence of a hostile faction known as the Islamic State's West Africa Province.
edges.context: Boko Haram split in 2016, creating a hostile faction called Islamic State's West Africa Province.

edges.evidence_quote: It has long been alleged that Boko Haram had a relationship with al-Qaeda.
edges.context: Boko Haram had a relationship with al-Qaeda, but al-Qaeda never officially accepted Boko Haram as an affiliate.

edges.evidence_quote: According to the UN Security Council listing of Boko Haram under the al-Qaeda sanctions regime in May 2014, the group 'has maintained a relationship with AQIM for training and material support purposes'.
edges.context: Boko Haram maintained a relationship with AQIM for training and material support.

edges.evidence_quote: The U.S. State Department designated Boko Haram and its offshoot Ansaru as terrorist organisations in November 2013.
edges.context: Ansaru is an offshoot of Boko Haram.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the group split, resulting in the emergence of a hostile faction known as the Islamic State's West Africa Province.
edges.context: Boko Haram split in 2016, creating a hostile faction.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The group formerly aligned itself with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant.
edges.context: Boko Haram was formerly aligned with ISIL.
